data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260511T011319-e8cc7cdf/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260511T011319-e8cc7cdf/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260511T011319-e8cc7cdf/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260511T011319-e8cc7cdf/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260511T011319-e8cc7cdf/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-d5615811">
    <title>feat(routing): bead-difficulty hint label sets initial ladder floor</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Today every bead starts at the configured global MinPower (or
  operator override). A 3-line typo fix and a 500-line refactor
  both start at the cheap tier, even when the bead title or
  description make it obvious the cheap tier will fail.

  ADR-024 P1 (cheapest first) is correct as a default, but cost
  optimization includes "use the right cheap" — when the operator
  knows a bead is hard, starting one ladder step up avoids a
  guaranteed-failed cheap attempt.

  Per ADR-024 Operator escape hatches subsection (sister bead
  ddx-451cdead), operators should be able to hint difficulty per
  bead via a label.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go investigationRetryInitialMinPower
    handles tier-hint reads but only for no_changes smart retry.
  - There's no general-purpose "start at this floor" hint
    mechanism for fresh beads.
  - The existing budget:<USD> label (sister bead ddx-8645c5d4)
    is the structural template; this bead adds a parallel
    tier:hint=<NAME> label.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add label parsing for tier:hint=<name> where <name> is one of:
    cheap, standard, smart, premium (or whatever ladder tier
    names are configured).
  - When a fresh bead is claimed, parse the label; if present,
    set the initial MinPower floor to that tier's MinPower.
  - If no label, use the configured default (current behavior).
  - If label is present but invalid (unknown tier name), log a
    warning and fall back to the default.
  - Compose with --min-power flag: the label sets a floor; the
    flag can raise it further but cannot lower below the label.
  - Document in CLAUDE.md (or operator docs): tier:hint=<name>
    label semantics.

  Tests:
     - TestTierHint_StartsAtLabelFloor
     - TestTierHint_NoLabel_UsesDefault
     - TestTierHint_InvalidLabel_FallsBackToDefault
     - TestTierHint_FlagRaisesFloorAboveLabel

NON-SCOPE
  - Auto-detection of bead difficulty from content (separate
    concern; this is operator-hint-only).
  - Tier-name validation against the configured ladder (operator
    is responsible for matching configured tier names).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-451cdead (ADR-024 amendment) Operator escape
    hatches subsection — this bead implements the tier-hint label.
  - Sister bead ddx-cf79f10c (no_changes stepwise) — uses the
    same hint mechanism for retries; this bead generalizes it
    to fresh claims.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Label tier:hint=<name> is parsed during bead claim; <name> resolves to the configured ladder tier's MinPower.
2. Fresh-bead dispatch uses the resolved MinPower as the initial floor when the label is present.
3. Without the label, dispatch uses the existing default (no behavior change).
4. Invalid label (unknown tier name) falls back to default with a warning event.
5. --min-power flag composes correctly: it can raise the floor but not lower it below the label-derived floor.
6. CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/) documents the tier:hint=<name> label semantics.
7. TestTierHint_StartsAtLabelFloor, TestTierHint_NoLabel_UsesDefault, TestTierHint_InvalidLabel_FallsBackToDefault, TestTierHint_FlagRaisesFloorAboveLabel all pass.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="b137ccaac946de99ce8304da203cf0133c6c47dc" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260511T011319-e8cc7cdf"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed successfully. All 9 ACs verified:

1. ✅ `ParseBeadTierHintLabel` parses `tier:hint=<name>` from labels — `cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go`
2. ✅ Fresh-bead dispatch uses the resolved MinPower floor — `investigationRetryInitialMinPower` in `agent_execute_loop_escalation.go`
3. ✅ No label → uses `baseMinPower` unchanged (`TestTierHint_NoLabel_UsesDefault`)
4. ✅ Invalid tier name → `HasBeadTierHintLabel` detects it, warns to stderr, falls back (`TestTierHint_InvalidLabel_FallsBackToDefault`)
5. ✅ `--min-power` composes as `max(labelFloor, baseMinPower)` (`TestTierHint_FlagRaisesFloorAboveLabel`)
6. ✅ Documented in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` section 7
7. ✅ All 4 tests pass: `TestTierHint_StartsAtLabelFloor`, `TestTierHint_NoLabel_UsesDefault`, `TestTierHint_InvalidLabel_FallsBackToDefault`, `TestTierHint_FlagRaisesFloorAboveLabel`
8. ✅ `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green`
9. ✅ `lefthook run pre-commit passes`
